questions.prompt: What's the default match type for most negative keywords?

choices.text: Negative phrase. Negative phrase blocks queries containing your phrase in that order without over-blocking semantic neighbors. Negative broad can over-filter — adding 'cheap' as negative broad blocks both 'cheap running shoes' (intended) and 'cheapest insurance' (collateral damage). Negative exact under-filters — only blocks the exact query, misses variants. Default to negative phrase; escalate to negative broad only when confident; use negative exact rarely (a single specific query you want gone).

questions.explanation: Negative phrase by default. Broad over-filters; exact under-filters.

questions.prompt: Account running broad match for 30 days, spend looks bloated. First thing to do?

choices.text: Open the Search Terms report, sort by impressions descending, scan the top queries for ones that don't match target intent (jobs, free, info-only, wrong category, competitor brand drift), add them as negatives at the appropriate level — usually negative phrase. A 15-minute weekly session catches 90% of waste. Skipping this audit is how broad-match accounts bleed money. Privacy aggregation hides long-tail queries but you'll see the spend-relevant majority. Don't kill broad match before doing the negatives work — it's almost always fixable through filtering.

questions.explanation: Search Terms report → add negative phrases. Don't kill broad; filter it.
